message:
test(fcp-conformance/smooth-pacer): pin SmoothPacer minimum-interval pacing semantics (br-z7td4)

SmoothPacer is the third RateLimiter primitive (alongside TokenBucket
and LeakyBucket which are already pinned at conformance level). It
enforces a pure inter-request spacing — no capacity, no burst, just
"wait at least N between requests". Zero conformance coverage today
despite the documented saturating-corner-case constructors that
defend against misconfiguration:
- from_rate(NaN | <=0) -> Duration::MAX (never acquirable)
- from_rate(infinite) -> Duration::ZERO (no spacing)

Adds 11 tests against the public fcp_ratelimit::SmoothPacer API:

- first_acquire_always_succeeds_regardless_of_interval
- second_acquire_within_min_interval_is_rejected
- acquire_after_min_interval_succeeds
- from_rate_zero_yields_never_acquirable_pacer (defensive guard:
  misconfigured rate must not collapse to "always allow")
- from_rate_negative_yields_never_acquirable_pacer
- from_rate_nan_yields_never_acquirable_pacer
- from_rate_infinite_yields_no_delay_pacer (consecutive acquires
  must succeed with no spacing)
- wait_time_decreases_as_elapsed_time_grows
- reset_clears_last_request_so_next_acquire_succeeds_immediately
- acquire_returns_wait_exceeded_when_max_wait_too_short (the
  deadline contract callers depend on)
- consecutive_paced_requests_honour_min_interval (pacing is per-pair,
  not just from the first request)

All 11 pass locally in 0.07s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
ratelimit_smooth_pacer_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
